description: Updates an existing Opportunity's stage and probability with validation, edit access check, and optimistic concurrency control. Requires two-step confirmation before execution. Enforces CRUD permissions and field-level security.

OpportunityId.description: Opportunity ID (18 characters, required). Must be a valid Opportunity the user has edit access to.

StageName.description: New opportunity stage (required). Must be one of the allowed stages: Prospecting, Qualification, Proposal, Negotiation, Closed Won, or Closed Lost.

salesforceUserId.description: Salesforce User ID (15 or 18 characters) for authorization enforcement. REQUIRED for security.

sessionId.description: Optional chat session ID for tracking multi-turn conversations

confirmationToken.description: Confirmation token generated during preview step. Required to prevent replay attacks.

outputValues.description: Output values from the Flow execution

requestId.description: Unique request ID for tracking and support

examples.name: Move ACME renewal to Negotiation stage
Access denied - user cannot edit Opportunity

output.error: Flow execution failed: Opportunity not found or you do not have access to edit it
You've reached the daily limit of 50 for this action. Try again tomorrow.

whenToUse: When the user explicitly requests to update an Opportunity's stage
When the user wants to move a deal forward in the sales process
When the user wants to mark an Opportunity as won or lost
As a follow-up action after searching for an Opportunity
bestPractices: Always show a preview of the proposed changes before executing
Require explicit user confirmation via the two-step confirmation flow
Validate that the OpportunityId exists and the user has edit access to it
Show the current stage and the new stage in the preview
Provide clear feedback about what will be changed
Include a link to the updated Opportunity in the success message
If user doesn't provide Probability, let the Flow use the default for that stage
limitations: Maximum 50 updates can be performed per user per day (configurable via ActionEnablement metadata)
User must have Edit permission on the Opportunity object
User must have edit access to the specific Opportunity (owner, sharing rules, etc.)
StageName must be a valid value from the org's Opportunity Stage picklist
Action can be disabled by administrators via ActionEnablement metadata
Optimistic concurrency: if Opportunity is modified by another user between lookup and update, the update may fail
confirmationFlow: 1. Agent proposes updating an Opportunity stage with specific values
2. LWC displays preview modal showing current stage → new stage
4. On confirm, LWC generates confirmation token and calls /action endpoint
5. Action Lambda validates token, checks rate limits, and executes Flow
6. Flow looks up Opportunity to verify edit access, then updates
7. LWC displays success message with link to updated Opportunity

securityNotes.authorization: This action enforces Salesforce CRUD permissions and sharing rules. Users can only update Opportunities if they have Edit permission on the Opportunity object and edit access to the specific record (owner, sharing rules, etc.).
securityNotes.confirmation: Two-step confirmation is REQUIRED. The action will fail if confirmationToken is missing or invalid.
securityNotes.rateLimiting: Rate limits are enforced per user per day. Default limit is 50 updates per day, configurable via ActionEnablement metadata.
securityNotes.auditLogging: All action executions are logged to AI_Action_Audit__c custom object with user ID, inputs (hashed if PII detected), success status, and timing metrics.
securityNotes.dataPrivacy: All communication occurs over private networking (PrivateLink). No data traverses the public internet.
securityNotes.killSwitch: Administrators can disable this action immediately by setting Enabled__c=false in ActionEnablement metadata.
securityNotes.concurrency: The Flow uses optimistic concurrency control. It looks up the Opportunity first to verify it exists and the user has access, then performs the update. If another user modifies the Opportunity between lookup and update, the update may fail with a concurrency error.

general: Always show preview before executing actions
Only suggest this action when user explicitly requests to update an Opportunity stage
Gather required information (OpportunityId, new StageName) before proposing the action
If user doesn't provide an OpportunityId, search for it first using retrieve_knowledge tool
Show both the current stage and the new stage in the preview
Use natural language to describe what will be changed
examplePrompts: Move the ACME renewal to Negotiation stage
Change the stage to Proposal/Price Quote
Move this deal to the next stage
responseFormat: When proposing the action, say: 'I can update the Opportunity "[Name]" from [Current Stage] to [New Stage]. Would you like me to proceed?'
After successful update, say: 'I've updated the Opportunity "[Name]" to [New Stage]. You can view it here: [link]'
If rate limited, say: 'You've reached the daily limit for updating Opportunities. You can make more updates tomorrow.'
If access denied, say: 'You don't have permission to edit this Opportunity. It may be owned by another user or outside your sharing rules.'
If validation fails, say: 'I need more information to update the Opportunity: [missing fields]'
NOTE: Due to confirmationToken requirement, this action currently requires the LWC component (ascendixAiSearch) to execute. Direct Agentforce invocation will fail until a wrapper is implemented.
stageProgression: Understand typical stage progression: Prospecting → Qualification → Proposal → Negotiation → Closed Won/Lost
When user says 'move to next stage', infer the next logical stage based on current stage
When user says 'mark as won', use 'Closed Won' stage
When user says 'mark as lost', use 'Closed Lost' stage
